text:
There was once a young, aspiring author who lived in a small town. She loved to write stories more than anything else but had no means to publish them. One day, while wandering through the local botanical garden, she chanced upon an old, dusty bookshop that had just opened its doors. Inside, shelves crowded with books filled every nook. She wandered through until her eyes fell upon a peculiar book adorned with illustrations of mysterious creatures.

The book's cover depicted a creature unlike anything she'd seen: an amalgamation of elephant and insect, its trunk entwined with antennae. It cooed sweetly in a language she didn't understand. Fascinated, she picked it up.

As she opened it, a gentle voice hummed from within. "What a beautiful creature you've found. It's a 'Harpyfly.'"

The voice belonged to an old man who was an accomplished bee whisperer and custodian of these rare insects. He took pity on the young writer
